extraction.one_line_summary: DUMOULIN Tom won the Stage 20 time trial by one second over FROOME Chris, securing second overall while THOMAS Geraint clinched overall victory.
tactical_patterns: DUMOULIN Tom rode without power meter or time splits, winning on instinct alone—suggests over-reliance on data can be detrimental.
FROOME Chris's podium resurrection via TT reveals late-race form peaking when GC pressure removed, lost second by equipment/pacing margins.
Top three TT performers matched exact GC podium—time trialing separated contenders from pretenders when climbing couldn't.
THOMAS Geraint rode defensively once hearing splits showed safety margin, prioritizing GC over stage win despite strong form.

rider_signals.DUMOULIN Tom: positive: Won without telemetry or splits, beating FROOME by one second suggests peak time trial instinct when racing angry.
rider_signals.FROOME Chris: positive: Second place TT performance with no GC pressure shows superior form arrives when tactical burden lifted.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Controlled TT ride backing off when splits showed safety, demonstrates mature GC management over stage-hunting ambition.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: negative: Lost podium to FROOME's TT by significant margin, revealing time trial as exploitable weakness despite climbing strength.
